message:
Last winter, my family group chat absolutely *came alive* when that ice storm rolled in. My mom was sending photos of her stockpiled soup. My brother was joking about becoming a professional snow-day pancake chef. My sister was triple-checking that everyone had flashlights. And me? I was just grateful we'd actually talked through the basics before things got real.

Turns out, a little prep beforehand means less panic and way more laughs when a storm's actually happening. Plus, everyone knew where to find what they needed without me having to coordinate a frantic 3am search party.

Do you and your family have your own funny preparedness quirks or group chat moments? I'd love to hear them! 😄
